creator: portmaster@bsdforge.com
text:
The message sent by your MX is BS. I've run hundreds of MX's, for
decades, and the message you (your RBL) present is near completely
useless, as it provides little recourse for it's recipient(s). You
mean that even tho I currently run over 50 MX's on my leased block
of IP's, I need register for an address at some phoney free mail
service. *Just* so I can tell you the people that run your chosen
RBL are a bunch of lazy *******s? What do you mean I can't send mail
to postmaster@, or abuse@? Precious few understand the work, and
maintenance required to run a SPAMLESS MX, as I do. But I have to
tell you, IMHO this seems like a bunch of nonsense.
For the record; had your chosen RBL been doing their job, they would
have consulted ARIN prior blocking swaths of the internet. Had they
done so, they would have discovered that I have leased my current /19
for ~ a decade, and during that time, not one abusive packet has ever
left that block. That block is: 24.113.32.0/19
You can easily verify this information through ARIN via
https://whois.arin.net/rest/net/NET-24-113-32-0-1 - see the *reassigned*?
and
https://whois.arin.net/rest/customer/C01179334.html
the range covers 24.113.32.0 - 24.113.63.255

Sorry for the venting, but I've been working with *BSD for decades,
and I'm on ~10 FreeBSD mailing lists, and maintain nearly 100 ports.
So I found this mail BS pretty [unnecessarily] frustrating.

Thank you for all your time, and consideration in this matter.

Chris Hutchinson

reject message follows

The original message was received at Tue, 12 Jan 2016 14:27:48 -0800 (PST)
from localhost [127.0.0.1]

   ----- The following addresses had permanent fatal errors -----
<freebsd-hackers@freebsd.org>
    (reason: 550-5.7.1 Service unavailable; client [24.113.41.81] blocked using zen.spamhaus.org)

   ----- Transcript of session follows -----
... while talking to mx1.freebsd.org.:
>>> RCPT To:<freebsd-hackers@freebsd.org>
<<< 550-5.7.1 Service unavailable; client [24.113.41.81] blocked using zen.spamhaus.org
<<< 550 5.7.1 in case of permanent delivery errors (e.g. 5XX SMTP errors) please send your problem report from a non-blocked location (e.g. gmail/yahoo) to postmaster@FreeBSD.org and include the following information: time (Jan 12 22:27:19) and client (24.113.41.81).
550 5.1.1 <freebsd-hackers@freebsd.org>... User unknown
>>> DATA
<<< 554-5.5.1 Error: no valid recipients
<<< 554 5.5.1 in case of permanent delivery errors (e.g. 5XX SMTP errors) please send your problem report from a non-blocked location (e.g. gmail/yahoo) to postmaster@FreeBSD.org and include the following information: time (Jan 12 22:27:19) and client (24.113.41.81).
##############
postmaster@FreeBSD.org should work for *anybody*, at least those whom
haven't abused your MX.
##############
